title: IPv6 and Modern Programmable Networks · Operations Brief: Net Ipv6 Autoconfiguration
summary: Extend the branch design for IPv6, centralized policy, overlays, zero trust, SASE, and infrastructure as code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net ipv6 autoconfiguration increment in a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain SLAAC, router advertisements, DHCPv6 choices, neighbor discovery, and default-gateway learning.
Distinguish global unicast, link-local, unique local, multicast, anycast, loopback, and unspecified IPv6 addresses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
